core_mechanism: nonlinear two-path interferometric parameter estimation, in which a nonlinear self-coupling among N interacting elements makes the accumulated relative phase depend super-linearly on N, so the estimation uncertainty of the coupling strength scales faster than 1/N
brief_reason: The abstract maps the evolving two-mode system onto a Ramsey interferometer containing a nonlinear medium and shows the interatomic scattering-strength uncertainty scales as 1/N^{7/5}, beating the 1/N (Heisenberg) bound achievable with a linear interferometer.
